Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results state: 'An SQM gene, Rch08d0024 (designated RcSQM)...'.

Applied_Species.reason: RcSQM was identified from and tested in Rehmannia chingii, though the functional assay was done in N. benthamiana. As a field value of R. chingii, this is supported.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper is on Rehmannia chingii and RcSQM is an R. chingii gene.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: TMS11 is the individual/population used for the genome/reference plant, but the paper does not explicitly tie RcSQM functional assay material to a named variety 'TMS11' in this gene-specific context.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: TMS11 is the reference individual/population for the study, but this exact source-variety assignment for RcSQM is not explicitly stated.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: RcSQM was tested for possible involvement in aucubin-to-catalpol conversion; catalpol is the terminal metabolite relevant to this claim.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Catalpol is described as the predominant iridoid glycoside.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states catalpol has neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, anti-apoptotic, antitumor, antioxidant, and antifibrotic effects.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show highest total iridoid glycosides in leaves; among finer tissues tender shoots are highest, followed by veinless leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper states no catalpol was detected after transient RcSQM expression in N. benthamiana with aucubin, indicating RcSQM is unlikely involved in aucubin epoxidation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by candidate identification, tissue expression analysis, transient expression in N. benthamiana with aucubin substrate, and metabolite detection showing no catalpol (Supplementary Fig. 17).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper discusses SE/SQM and identifies RcSQM as an SQM gene; squalene monooxygenase/squalene epoxidase-related designation is consistent with the text.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states RcSQM showed relatively higher expression in leaf tissues, roots, and corollas, but overall SE/SQM expression was low across examined tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same expression pattern is reported for RcSQM in R. chingii tissues.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper says RcSQM coding sequence was cloned into a pMDC83 overexpression vector; no promoter is specified in this RcSQM section.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Functional testing used aucubin as substrate in transiently expressed N. benthamiana leaves.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized points match the Results: Rch08d0024 was the relatively more expressed SQM candidate, transient expression failed to produce catalpol, and RcSQM was excluded as the aucubin epoxidase candidate.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable interpretation, but the paper does not explicitly discuss RcSQM's engineering value as a negative target to avoid.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq data are reported, and all sequencing data including RNA-seq were deposited under BioProject PRJCA032868.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says it had been speculated that SE or SQM may catalyze aucubin epoxidation, and their experiment showed RcSQM did not do so.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results explicitly identify RcG10H as Rch13d0510.

Applied_Species.reason: RcG10H originates from R. chingii and was functionally tested in yeast; the gene's applied biological context is R. chingii.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The gene is from Rehmannia chingii.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The study plant is from TMS11, but the gene-specific application field does not explicitly name TMS11 for RcG10H assays.

Source_Species.reason: RcG10H is a Rehmannia chingii gene.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: TMS11 is the genome individual/population, but the gene source variety is not directly stated for RcG10H.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The functional assay demonstrated formation of 10-hydroxygeraniol.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: 10-hydroxygeraniol is an iridoid-pathway intermediate derived from monoterpenoid metabolism; this classification is directly inferable from the pathway description.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper places 10-hydroxygeraniol in the iridoid pathway leading to iridoid glycosides such as catalpol.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: RcG10H is discussed as a key enzyme in the iridoid pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Geraniol 10-hydroxylation is part of the early iridoid pathway described in the paper.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: This reaction is upstream relative to catalpol formation in the described pathway.

Enzyme_Name.reason: RcG10H is explicitly identified as geraniol 10-hydroxylase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states RcG10H belongs to the CYP76 group.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Geraniol feeding was used in yeast to test RcG10H activity.

Primary_Product.reason: The product was identified as 10-hydroxygeraniol by HPLC and GC-MS.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states G10H hydroxylates geraniol at the C-10 position to produce 10-hydroxygeraniol.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: 10-hydroxygeraniol is a downstream precursor/intermediate in the iridoid pathway leading to catalpol.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly calls G10H a key enzyme in the iridoid pathway, though not rate-limiting.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results demonstrate RcG10H has geraniol 10-hydroxylase activity.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by heterologous expression in S. cerevisiae WAT11, geraniol feeding, and HPLC plus GC-MS identification.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RcG10H is included among Group I P450s predominantly expressed in leaf, veinless leaf, and leaf vein, with lower expression in roots and stems.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same tissue expression pattern in R. chingii is shown/described.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper states RcG10H CDS was cloned into pYeDP60 and expressed in yeast, but does not specify the promoter in the field wording.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Geraniol feeding in yeast culture is explicitly described.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported: clustering with candidate P450s, leaf-enriched expression pattern, and conversion of geraniol to authentic 10-hydroxygeraniol in yeast.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible application inference, but the paper does not explicitly discuss RcG10H engineering/stacking strategies.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq is used in the study and sequencing data are deposited under PRJCA032868.

Other_Important_Info.reason: RcG10H was functionally validated, but calling it a 'validated positive reference among candidate P450s' is interpretive rather than explicitly stated.

Applied_Species.reason: RcCYP72H7 was tested in both Nicotiana benthamiana transient assays and Rehmannia chingii leaves.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both species names appear in the paper in connection with RcCYP72H7 assays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: TMS11 is the R. chingii study individual/population, but the paper does not explicitly state RcCYP72H7 transient assays in R. chingii were specifically done in 'TMS11'; N. benthamiana variety is not given.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: TMS11 is the study accession/population, but not explicitly assigned as the source variety for RcCYP72H7 in the gene-specific Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Catalpol is described as an iridoid glycoside.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper lists catalpol's neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, anti-apoptotic, antitumor, antioxidant, and antifibrotic effects.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show highest total iridoid glycoside accumulation in leaves, especially tender shoots and then veinless leaves.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: RcCYP72H7 is identified in iridoid glycoside/catalpol biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper specifically places RcCYP72H7 in the downstream aucubin-to-catalpol part of catalpol biosynthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: RcCYP72H7 catalyzes the final epoxidation step from aucubin to catalpol.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper identifies RcCYP72H7 as an aucubin epoxidase and designates it catalpol synthase (RcCS).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: RcCYP72H7 is described as a member of the CYP72A family.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The fluorescence signal of RcCYP72H7-GFP was distributed in the endoplasmic reticulum.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Aucubin was fed in the transient expression assays and RcCYP72H7 acted on it.

Primary_Product.reason: A product matching catalpol was detected only for RcCYP72H7.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The Discussion explicitly states RcCYP72H7 catalyzes epoxidation of aucubin at the C7-C8 position to convert it into catalpol.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: RcCYP72H7 directly forms the target product catalpol from aucubin.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is clearly a terminal determinant of catalpol formation, but 'key control step strongly supported' is stronger than what the paper explicitly demonstrates; rate-limiting was not tested.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper shows RcCYP72H7 catalyzes aucubin epoxidation to catalpol and that transient expression in R. chingii leaves increased catalpol content.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported: candidate screening, N. benthamiana transient expression with aucubin feeding, LC-MS/MS, GFP fusion western blot, active GFP fusion assay, transient expression in R. chingii leaves, HPLC quantification, and subcellular localization.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RcCYP72H7 is among CYP72 cluster genes with high expression in leaf tissues and lower expression in roots/root tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: In R. chingii it is leaf-enriched; in N. benthamiana it was transiently expressed in infiltrated leaves.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper reports pMDC83 overexpression vector for candidate gene assays and explicitly states 35S promoter for GFP fusion constructs.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Aucubin feeding in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves and the stated transient-expression conditions in R. chingii are described in Methods.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is directly supported by the Results, including chromosome 8 cluster membership, exclusive catalpol formation among seven candidates, active GFP fusion, 55.07% catalpol increase, ER localization, and designation as RcCS.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study implies utility for genetic improvement and pathway engineering, but the detailed biofortification/stacking strategy wording is not directly stated.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper does not assess off-target CYP72 effects or growth/yield penalties; the field is mostly absence-of-evidence commentary.

Omics_Data.reason: Genome and RNA-seq data are central to the study; BioProject PRJCA032868 and genome assembly accession GWHFIKC00000000.1 are explicitly provided.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says RcCYP72H7 was designated RcCS and that this reveals epoxidase activity within the CYP72 family, expanding known P450 functions.
